channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wall Street Journal Warns Trump Family Financial Gains Could Create Political Risks for Republicans
item.description: A Wall Street Journal editorial board piece has raised concerns about Donald Trump’s financial activities and their potential political consequences for Republicans. According to the editorial, Trump ...

item.title: Municipalities face pressure as electricity tariff hikes strain affordability and sustainability
item.description: Municipalities across South Africa are facing increasing pressure as electricity tariff hikes place strain on both local government finances and residents’ ability to pay. From Wednesday, residents in...

item.title: Tanzania Highlights Job Creation and Local Participation in EACOP Project Implementation
item.description: The Tanzanian government has praised the progress and local involvement in the East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project, describing it as a major driver of employment, skills development, and p...

item.title: WA property developers take accounting firm to Supreme Court over alleged tax mismanagement
item.description: Two prominent Western Australian property entrepreneurs have initiated legal proceedings in the WA Supreme Court against their long-time accounting firm, HLB Mann Judd, alleging widespread professiona...

item.title: Three residents still living in Granville SRO after end-of-June relocation deadline
item.description: Three residents continue to live at the Luugat single-room occupancy (SRO) building on Granville Street in Vancouver despite a government-set deadline of June 30 to relocate all tenants. According to ...

item.title: Where to watch Australia vs Egypt in the FIFA World Cup knockout match across Australia
item.description: Australia’s Socceroos are set for a crucial Round of 32 knockout match against Egypt in the FIFA World Cup 2026™, with kick-off scheduled for 4am AEST on Saturday. The match, being played in Dallas, T...

item.title: LSK warns government over enforcement of Finance Act 2026 and signals possible legal challenge
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has raised concerns over the implementation of the Finance Act 2026, cautioning the government that it may initiate court action if state agencies engage in administrati...

item.title: James Van Der Beek’s final performance in Amazon series Elle moves fans after his death
item.description: James Van Der Beek’s final screen appearance has been released in the Amazon Prime Video series Elle, drawing an emotional response from viewers following his death at the age of 48 from cancer earlie...

item.title: Ambassador Kairuki calls for Africa to become a creator and leader in artificial intelligence development
item.description: Tanzania’s High Commissioner to the United Kingdom, Ambassador Mbelwa Kairuki, has called on African countries to move away from only consuming technology and instead take an active role in shaping an...

item.title: Study shows shared brain mechanism between Buddhist jhāna meditation and Pentecostal speaking in tongues
item.description: This article talks about a research work that looked into how two very different spiritual practices—Buddhist jhāna meditation and Christian Pentecostal speaking in tongues—may actually share similar ...

item.title: Critique of collective guilt narratives in European historical memory politics
item.description: The article is a provocative opinion piece that challenges the idea of collective historical guilt, particularly in relation to Germany’s remembrance of Nazi-era crimes. The author argues that legal a...

item.title: Badenoch criticises UK defence funding gap amid dispute over £15bn investment plan
item.description: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch has criticised Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer over what she described as a significant funding gap in the UK’s new defence investment plan, arguing that it leaves an...

item.title: Deadly Russian missile and drone barrage hits Kyiv, leaving at least 13 dead
item.description: Russian forces carried out a large-scale overnight assault on Ukraine’s capital, Kyiv, launching hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles that struck residential areas and critical infrastructure. Ac...

item.title: Vehicle torched by protesters in Imphal East amid rising tensions in Manipur
item.description: A telecom vehicle carrying communication-related equipment was set on fire by protesters in Imphal East district of Manipur amid escalating ethnic and law-and-order tensions in the region. According t...

item.title: Climate Minister Musadik Malik highlights Indus Waters Treaty dispute as a global water governance issue
item.description: Pakistan’s Climate Change Minister Musadik Malik has described the ongoing Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) dispute as a wider global challenge concerning water governance and the rights of downstream countr...

item.title: ICPC secures court order to detain former Minister Uche Nnaji over alleged certificate forgery investigation
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) has obtained a Federal High Court order permitting it to detain former Minister of Science and Technology, Uche Nnaji, fo...

item.title: DA leader Geordin Hill-Lewis outlines vision for political and economic transition in address to party members
item.description: Democratic Alliance (DA) leader Geordin Hill-Lewis addressed party members and supporters on Thursday, setting out what he described as a critical political and economic transition needed in South Afr...

item.title: Political standoff between Congress and BRS over Telangana Gurukul procurement allegations escalates in Hyderabad
item.description: A political confrontation between Telangana’s ruling Congress party and the opposition Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) intensified in Hyderabad, centering on allegations of irregularities in procurement ...

item.title: Poilievre criticizes Carney’s economic record, calling public perception an ‘illusion’
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre has argued that Prime Minister Mark Carney’s strong public approval is based on perception rather than measurable economic results. In an interview with podcaster ...

item.title: Manila Implements Road Closures and Alternative Routes for INC Rally on July 2
item.description: Authorities in Manila have announced widespread road closures and traffic rerouting measures on Thursday, July 2, in response to a rally organized by the religious group Iglesia ni Cristo (INC) at Liw...
